content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which require no extra training or labeled data, can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to eliminate the blind spots that come from single-perspective iterative self-feedback. This creates a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training while fixing its common failure mode of getting stuck in local optimization loops caused by consistent single-perspective feedback.
